summary: Namespace-abuse wrapper of vibe-kanban that downloads and executes binaries from an unrelated personal GitHub repo
details: This package reproduces the name, README, and branding of the upstream vibe-kanban project (BloopAI) and lists the author as 'bloop', but is published under an unrelated user scope. On invocation, bin/cli.js constructs URLs of the form https://github.com/tianweilong/deploy-center/releases/download/<tag>/<asset> and downloads platform-specific executables, then runs them via spawn/execSync. On Windows it executes the downloaded installer with NSIS silent-install flags (`"${installerPath}" /S /D="${installDir}"`), giving the unrelated publisher full code execution under the running user. The release publisher (`tianweilong/deploy-center`) has no documented relationship to the impersonated upstream, no signature/hash verification of the downloaded binaries is performed, and the binaries themselves are opaque and unauditable. Additionally, package.json declares `"main": "index.js"` but no index.js is shipped — a manifest/tarball mismatch consistent with a hasty namespace-abuse publish. Anyone running `npx @vino.tian/vibe-kanban` ends up executing arbitrary native code from a third-party personal GitHub repo under the cover of a well-known tool's name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
